response.justification: Bogdan is a seasoned generalist engineer with roughly 20 years of experience across web, mobile, embedded, and data systems, and his recent role touching MCP frameworks and agentic workflows shows an emerging interest in AI. However, this role demands a director-level individual contributor with recognized, deep AI expertise — the kind evidenced by built AI platforms, published research, or industry thought leadership — none of which the candidate demonstrates. His AI work is both very recent (April 2025) and described at an infrastructure-tooling level rather than strategic product or platform leadership. He lacks an advanced degree in an AI-related field, has no demonstrated experience advising enterprise customers on AI/cloud strategy, and his profile reads as a strong senior/staff engineer rather than a principal capable of shaping organizational AI direction.
strengths: ~20 years of diverse engineering experience spanning web, embedded, high-throughput data systems, and mobile — comfortably clears the 15-year minimum.
Entrepreneurial track record as founder of GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform in active use by NGOs and conservation professionals.
Current role at re:cinq includes hands-on work with AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators, signaling a deliberate pivot toward agentic AI tooling.
concerns: No recognized depth in AI — no published research, no shipped AI product at scale, and AI experience only begins in April 2025; far below the 'demonstrated products or platforms built with AI' bar the role sets.

Seniority gap is significant: the role is director-level IC focused on strategic influence and customer advisory, whereas the candidate's history is hands-on senior/staff engineering with no evidence of executive-level stakeholder engagement.

No cloud specialization at the depth required — AWS CDK usage at Optilyz and Kubernetes for a side project do not constitute the cloud platform expertise expected of someone advising strategic cloud customers.
